review_text: We stay here every year with friends. Absolutely love this park and area. Now it is not run by NRMA it doesn't seem as good.

The only thing for me share here is the word
"Perfect indeed"
👍
Great location great service

While most aspects of our stay were acceptable, we are extremely dissatisfied and concerned about a serious safety incident that occurred during our visit.

A group of young individuals, clearly not registered guests, entered the campsite on electric bikes at high speed. They recklessly rode through the area, attempted to throw objects at us, verbally abused us, and even tried to physically attack one of our friends. This behavior is dangerous, aggressive, and completely unacceptable.

It is evident that there is a major failure in site management and security. Unauthorized individuals are able to enter the campsite freely, putting paying guests at risk.

This is particularly alarming given that many families staying here have young children aged 3–10. Incidents like this create a highly unsafe environment and are simply intolerable.

We paid for a safe and enjoyable holiday experience. Instead, our stay was disrupted and put at risk by individuals who should never have had access to the premises in the first place.

We expect immediate and concrete action to be taken, including stricter access control, increased security presence, and measures to prevent unauthorized entry.

If this matter is not addressed promptly and seriously, we will have no hesitation in escalating this complaint further through formal channels and public platforms.

It’s the only Caravan Park on the Northside of Sydney, so you don’t have many options.  Located right in Narrabeen Lagoon and a  short walk over to the beach the position is sensational.  The sites aren’t great with big pine trees scattered everywhere and unlevel sites.  The facilities are aging.  It has just been taken over by BIG4 so will be interesting to see if some money is invested.  Also no dogs which is a shame as it’s a very big park and they certainly have the room for a pet friendly area

The location is genuinely unbeatable. close to everything you could need including a grocery store, great local café with excellent food and even the Rockpool all walking distance.  It makes exploring the area incredibly easy without having to travel far. The park itself is well kept clean and comfortable with facilities that are clearly maintained with care. The staff were welcoming and friendly, which made for easy check in. A solid, well-run park in a fantastic location.

Great location next to the beach with nearby restaurants and some facilities for kids, but unfortunately the overall experience was very disappointing.

Customer service was poor — we requested multiple times (via message and phone) to have our group camping sites allocated together, but received no response or confirmation. Upon arrival, all sites were far apart, even under the same booking, and staff refused to help or acknowledge our previous requests.

The campsite condition was also unacceptable. Our allocated sites were dirt with small rocks, which damaged our tent and became very muddy and dirty, while other sites had much better artificial grass.

Most concerning was the safety. The park is very open, and at night groups of teenagers were riding e-bikes and bicycles at high speed through the campsite, creating serious risk for children. There were also incidents of verbal abuse and harassment targeting Asian campers. Although security was notified, the issue continued for two nights without being properly managed.

We recently stayed here with 6 families, including 11 young children aged 2–6, and had a very concerning experience.

On the first night around 8–9 pm, a group of teenagers approached our campsite asking for food. Even after we told them to leave, they refused, lingered around, and even sat on our chairs without permission. When we asked them to go, one of them punched my husband’s water bottle, which was intimidating and completely unacceptable. Their behaviour also escalated to racial harassment, including mocking our language. They were riding e-bikes at high speed through the campsite, which was extremely dangerous for young children.

Security and police were called. We were told these teenagers were not guests and had entered due to unrestricted public access. This is a serious failure in site security—anyone can walk into the camping area.

Shockingly, the same group returned on the second night. For the first time while camping, I felt unsafe and couldn’t sleep due to concerns for my children’s safety. The stress and lack of rest caused me to develop a strong headache the following day.

We also spoke to other Asian families staying nearby who experienced similar incidents. It appeared we were being specifically targeted.

This place has a major security and safety issue, especially for families with young kids. Until proper access control and security are in place, I would not recommend staying here.
Absolute wonderful place to bring your with a community feel and relaxed style plenty of activities and places to visit would easily recommend
Well Below average caravan park ||Security of guests low priority for management looting of sites accepted with no imediate action  taken council blamed for the security problems.

A great place but security does nothing about loud groups using the camp kitchens as they are "not closed" despite signs saying kitchen is 8am-10pm only and lights switching off at 10.30. Loud swearing is not acceptable at 12.30am especially in a park full of children. Smoking in front of cabins isn't fair either, smelling it wafting to the camp sites. Two nights here and two nights with this selfish behaviour. Why isn't security doing anything of course they must be able to patrol it. Then when it's over they are speeding off in their cars, obviously not even guests but parking in the place.

One of my favourite parks but was highly disappointed with the office girl I dealt with, I was assured I would have the same site for 7 days but on the 5th day at 1 pm while at Randwick race I received a call that I would have to move sites, I told them this was not possible as I was out for the day. She was insisting that I had to come back and move which I replied she must be joking. She replied “you’re the joke” I hung up and didn’t hear from them again
Random broken window but well handled by security staff. Great location. Very family friendly. Cabin itself could use some maintenance.
Awesome location and all the Big4 services u would want. We booked a cabin and were happy to have over  booked to allow some more space.  Couples cabins we saw were abit too basic for us.
Great location access to lake , beach , walking areas and shops . A safe family holiday destination.Friendly staff , clean villas and cabins ,local cafes and restaurants
My husband and I shared a long weekend and we're harassed by loads of boys knocking on the van yelling and chasing rabbits, we will not be coming back here.

Fantastic spot, but a warning for campers: avoid the low-lying sites! Ours flooded completely during a storm, and the soft ground combined with wind pulled our pegs right out. We actually had to abandon the tent at midnight to sleep at home. Lovely area, check your spot carefully before camping.

One of the best holiday parks I’ve been to. This is my second time and I will definitely be back. I have travelled across Australia and stayed in many holiday parks. So far this place has the best staff I’ve seen. So understanding and caring. We really felt welcomed as if we are at home.

Je ne comprends pas certains commentaire très négatifs sur cet établissement. Nous avons hésité et nous avons vraiment bien fait d’y aller! Nous devions rester deux nuits et avons pris une troisième nuit.
Le camping est très grand, très calme malgré la proximité de la route principale.
Beaucoup de pins, canards et lapins qui se promènent. Les emplacements sont en herbe synthétique avec une grande table de pic nic à chaque emplacement.
La plage et le lac sont littéralement à deux minutes de marche. Très très bel emplacement. Départs de balades, piscine rocheuse remplie par les marées, spot de surf très sympa, cafés à proximité et commerces, ainsi que le bus pour aller à Manly et Sydney rapidement.
Petit conseil : choisir les emplacements sud qui sont en bord de lac.
Également beaucoup d’activités pour enfants, location de vélos, toilettes et douches très propres. Laverie 4$ la machine et 1$ le sèche linge.

The location is great. We had the bunkhouse for two adults and two kids. The adults slept in bunks. There was sand on the floor, the door to the bathroom didn’t close properly, and there were no table or chairs inside. Not much room inside glad we were only there for the night

Détestable, le camping est mal entretenu.
Pour commencer, nous avons fait deux réservations et nous ne comprenons pas pourquoi sur le premier emplacement nous avons eu du gazon synthétique et sur le deuxième non. Les BBQ manquent de spatule, dans tous les campings il y en a, donc pourquoi ne pas en mettre à disposition ? Les douches sont dans un état lamentable (la clientèle est à l'image du camping : sale) et nous ne comprenons pas pourquoi les douches en haut du camping sont plus récentes et celles d'en bas sont veilles. Concernant les horaires pour le kitchen Camp et les laundry : nous ne comprenons pas pourquoi vous les fermez la nuit alors que vous pouvez totalement activer le code afin que personne d'extérieur n'y accède. Les emplacements sont vraiment inconfortables car ils sont collés, nous sommes tous entassés les uns sur les autres. Bref une clientèle irrespectueuse avec un camping mal entretenu. Il n'y a que la vue qui sauve un tout petit peu. Nous sommes très déçus.

camping ammenities.
Showers were not clean. Same piece of soap on the floor for 3 days. Shaved hair in the drain plugs.
Noisy well after 10.30pm
What a lovely place to visit. Really enjoyed the aria and what it had to offer. Narrabeen is beautiful.

Es ist ein toller Campingplatz. Die Nähe zu Sydney ist perfekt und easy wenn man mit Caravan reist so wie wir.|Parken und Reisen hat hervorragend geklappt. Eine Buskarte für 50 Dollar,  gültig für eine Woche. Wir haben sie mehr wie ausgenutzt.  Es lohnt sich.|Kleiner Tipp: ist das Parkhaus voll besetzt einfach an einer Nebenstraße parken - funktioniert gut.|Die Sanitäranlagen waren voll intakt. Leider haben sich nicht alle Gäste respektvoll verhalten. Es ist beschämend was so mancher dem Reinigungspersonal zumutet.|Wir können den Platz zu 100 % weiterempfehlen. Leider hatten wir keine Zeit für den Strand, aber auch hier eine perfekte Lage mit Strandnähe, man kann es erlaufen.

I stayed in a cabin for 14 nights, the location was excellent and the cabin was quiet at nighttime.  The reason for the poor rating  is that the cabin is in need of serious renovation, the entrance door was dirty and marked, the carpet was grotty and dirty ( I took pictures) and the fridge door badly marked. I did bring it to the attention of office staff who blamed the cleaning staff but I found the cleaning staff excellent.  The picture on the website does not reflect the actual cabin.  I also gave feedback when I checked out but got no response.

We loved the location the ground staff were amazing the staff and the desk not so welcoming at all we had a very large stain on our bed that look to be 💩 in went and told them at the desk thay come and changed it but the shower leaked everywhere aswell disappointed for what we pay i think need to put some money into doing some work on the cabins
美麗風景😎，湖區美麗👍，房屋樓下可拍車🛻

We went with our campervan for just 1 night. The office was very disorganised, sent us to 2 different sites that were already occupied. We ended up next to very, very noisy kids and chainsmokers on the other side. Both sets didn’t care about the quiet after 10pm rule or much else. The whole site was very litter heavy with plastic wrappers and crisps packages flying in the breeze. (Not good for all the wildlife!)
A lot of the bbq hot plates were out of service as were some of the hobs. The amenities were ok, quite new in fact.
The staff interactions we had were friendly, but this was the most expensive site by far we used,  and it also was the worst by far. We would not go back!

Fast and easy online check in. We went with a group and our sites were close so a big thank you to reception. The amenities were always clean and tidy. Lots of families and travelers enjoying this slice of heaven.
The only negative was the teenage party on the beach at 2am. Unfortunately our street was a thoroughfare for the very young, loud drunk kids coming through. It was obvious they are local and I’m hoping this was a once off as we’d like to come back however it was a  difficult night, particularly with a little one.
2 minuten lopen van strand, , goede verbinding met openbaar vervoer naar centrum Sydney
Love this place, NSW coastal getaway half an hour from Sydney. Perfect for international tourists with limited time, or Sydney families doing starter camping / getaways for kids.

Very warn park for the price they charge, amenities often dirty and cleaned at peak times in the morning making the other amenities block very busy |Not much for the kids to do on site and sites own rules not enforced other than a reminder text
Great location, right next to the water. Rooms were OK, clean. Nice vibe in the area.

Repeat visitors across Australia Day holiday. Love the location of the Lake side villas. The amenity is very good. The prices rose significantly this year with no improvements or update to villas. Hopefully this is addressed. Villas vary in equipment. Some have new, large fridge suitable for a family for a week, others much smaller. The pots and pans are mixed up between villas, just ask reception to sort it out. Kids activities during school holidays great - eg tie dye t shirts, decorate a cap, air dry clay craft. Movie nights. This location is amazing and we’ll be back.

Disappointed.  I booked in with friends and was allocated a site next to them.  I get here it couldn’t be any further away.  It’s a big park. I complained and they moved me closer, still heaps away.  Weekend plans ruined.  It’s a big effort to pack and go away.  We had plans and now cannot do it.  Group bookings just prepare for disappointment.
For $400+ a night, you would expect better than 1 ply toilet paper, filthy floors and mattresses that are impossible to sleep on.

Wir waren 2 Nächte (Ende Dez 2025) an diesem Campingplatz. Uns hat gut gefallen, dass er so nahe am Strand war. Zwischen den Plätzen gab es keine Sichtschutz oder Bäume, was wir in bei anderen Campingplätzen besser fanden. Auf dem Campingplatz hatte es viele Vögel und freilebende Kaninchen. Die Lage des Campingplatz war ruhig, aber da es keine Sichtschutz o.Ä. zu Platznachbaren habe eher unruhig und man hörte und sah die Nachbaren ständig. Toiletten waren okej bezüglich Sauberkeit.

Fantastic place for kids.
Good wheelchair access.
Happy, friendl6 area, highly recommend

The location is fabulous, but we were personally disappointed that we were not allowed fires, not even a charcoal cooker. We noticed other campers had bought firewood but left it behind. Unable to use it. It was fairly disappointing considering one of our plans was to make damper.

So glad we found this holiday park last minute when everything else was booked or overpriced. The room had all the basics, plus air conditioning and a balcony, which was a nice bonus. Camp kitchens and laundry rooms were close by and well set up.

The location is unbeatable — right next to the beach and also close to a lake, so you can come and go anytime. Local shops, cafés, and bus stops are all within walking distance.

We loved seeing wildlife around the park, and the place felt lively yet relaxing. It was busy, which says a lot about how popular it is. We really enjoyed our stay and would happily return again✨

We stayed recently over Christmas. Location is fabulous and the kids loved it. However the quality of the cabin was terrible. Dusty, not clean, feet were black within minutes of arriving. Beds weren’t made, bathroom mouldy. Beds were so uncomfortable. When raising concerns with staff at front desk we were told they don’t have time to make beds etc. absolutely not worth the money you pay to stay. Wrote to raise our concerns again and we were ignored with no acknowledgment. Sad really as we won’t be recommending this place again to anyone.
Relax

We had a beautiful time here, absolutely amazing location by the beach and lagoon. Photos are all within a 2-5 minute walk. There are also nice cafes and a supermarket in reasonable walking distance.

The open kitchen facilities are convenient, and the barista stand that’s open in the morning has delicious coffee and smoothies served by lovely people.

For a campground stay, bring headphones/earplugs and an eye cover if you’re a light sleeper.

This is my review for camping in a van

Pros:
Location is great, nice coastal vibe, walk across to the beach or walk to the local shops along lake.

Reception Wendy was friendly & patient

Bunny rabbits, ducks, kookaburras

The automatic hot water tap was a nice touch in the kitchens, saves time waiting for a plastic kettle to boil

Kitchens nice

Cons:
At 1st a nice beach view spot sounds like a good idea, but then I realised that it comes with the road that separates the beach to the camp spot, so I was overwhelmed by the sound of cars non stop, people walking past & also looking right at my open van, no shade.
Not very peaceful.

Construction was also underway at the beach, so you could hear all the machinery moving sand around

Shaded spots in the park look very close together

Showers were the worst! ☹️ A small head on the wall that you can't adjust or move around. A simple shower head replacement could fix it

Pricey
Very close to the beach

Initially booked for 4 nights but stayed 7. The park staff made it easy enough to extend our stay even though they were busy. Becoming a Big4 member was well worth it for the discounted stay. Was a great  location to get out on our stand up paddle boards.
Great family holiday location. Lots for the little ones to do. Very nice coffee cart team.

Location is fantastic — the beach and lagoon are within walking distance, and the shops are only a short drive away. We’ve visited every year for the past three years and have always loved the convenience.

However, this stay was disappointing.
Check-in was at 2pm, yet our room hadn’t been properly freshened up on arrival. We also found several cleanliness issues, including:

Mould on the blinds (the blinds were replaced after we mentioned it, but the glass behind them still wasn’t cleaned)

Dust on tables, windows, and power points

Someone else’s socks left in the drawers

Marks on the blankets

After such positive past stays, this was not the standard we expected

Great holiday park. Have been coming here for years with our van and also camped in tents and recently tried a bunkhouse which I can highly recommend. Park is always clean and is easy to get around . Never had a problem with security either. Is opposite north narrabeen beach and you can hear the waves at night when camping

Please BE AWARE!!!!
Be aware that your caravan site is not guaranteed - we were told that since it’s now a Big 4 caravan park, they have the right to change the original site that you booked at any time as they wish. I just had to write this review - hopefully this never happens to anyone else

We were physically in the office 3 weeks prior, verified that we had our Lakeside site booked - fully paid, yet when we arrived , we were candidly told that they moved us off the lakeside facing site to somewhere at the back with no consideration for the size of our caravan. Reception Service staff stated that this was their right and there was nothing we could do about it. The Manager Craig was of no help either - no recognition of the impact on patrons and how this affects their experience in the park

When we asked why we were relocated, it was because the person on the site wanted to extend for 1 more day even though our booking was for a week. As you can see on the attached photograph, the person was not even on the site when we arrived. This was no unforeseen circumstance or emergency - just poor park management

We agree with previous reviews that reception staff are very unwelcoming (Francesca and Judy) who just kept on spitting out rules that made no sense at all and they kept repeating that they have the right to move people whenever they want as they are not NRMA and things have changed. There was TOTAL lack of understanding for the needs of the patrons

It would be extra sad if this should happen when people book in order to be together and then they land up being split anywhere in the park - sending their holiday to ruins

A really great site that was large and open with a good size plot (we stayed on an en-suite camper site).

The en-suite facilities were clean and everything worked! (Unlike other places we have been to).

Plus with the bus stops down the road, you can easily get the 199 to Manly or the B1 straight to Sydney.

You can’t really go wrong going here in my view.

(There’s also loads of rabbits which was rather cute to see)

Amazing location, especially in the “Lakeview Villas” by the lagoon. Lovely views and easy access to the sandy lagoon shores and a short walk up towards Narrabeen Beach.

Villas themselves are fine, layout and amenities are as expected…but the furniture is badly worn and dirty. I hope they are replaced for the peak summer season when people will be paying an arm and a leg to stay here. BBQ area sill was also soiled badly with grease. Hilarious considering you are basically expected to do half the cleaning for them before you check out.

The new management of this once highly-desirable holiday park, which we would regularly frequent, have ensured we will never come back. Booking specific sites is now meaningless as they will simply put you where they want. If you're coming in a group you'll probably find yourself dispersed throughout the park. Book here if you have absolutely no preferences, are camping alone and favour disrespect over customer service.

Un beau camping situé en bordure d'un lac, calme malgré la présence d'une route proche. La mer est située à 5min de marche, avec une immense piscine d'eau de mer remplie par les marées. Nous avons apprécié notre séjour
Great spot! Close to ocean pool and surf beach.
Great park! Loads of fun and an awesome location.

Its got everything, towels, sheets & blankets, tea & coffee, its like a motel at much  less cost, dissapointing its no longer with NRMA because it came with better benefits but 'you can pay $50' to join Big 4 Perks to get a discount. Go figure....

Park is very nice kids love the waterslide and pool and the putt putt golf ,while we love the security of the park being fully fenced .
The main amenities block has had trouble with there septic system for the last two years in a row very bad smell and toilets have blocked occasionaly .
The amenities do need a good scrub lots of mould.
One of the main swings at the park remain broken although it was a very easy fix.
Lots of taps constantly leaking that need repairing.
And the power boxes on the sites need attention.
Staff in the office were very nice and helpful.
Stayed on a Southside site on the Lake.  Great spot.  Staff were okay.  Amenities were adequate but could use a complete refurbishment.

Location was great only brought down by Nicole the receptionist who decided my child could  pee themself when she declined giving the code for FAMILY room when the toilets were being cleaned which takes half a day because they were not a baby.

Booked a large powered site for 7 nights. Park over priced and over crowded. Toilets and showers were filthy. Front desk staff were surly and seemed more concerned in getting you to sign an acknowledgement that you have read and understand 2 A4 pages of park rules, yet there were no park staff on the ground enforcing them.
Seemed content to pile people on top of each other rather than provide some space and probably when possible.
Couldn’t recommend anymore. Nice family friendly holiday location. Very spacious, clean and tidy. Definitely recommend

SERVICE was absolutely terrible, each staff member we came across on the front desk from check in to check out were very unfriendly. They always looked miserable and cranky and certainly shown through their service to each guest in the office. They were rude and never smiled, I seen them treat all guest’s like this when I was in the office. Front office staff were big on hammering guests about all the rules within the park but nobody reinforcing these rules out and around the park. From the speed limit for cars in the park and kids on bikes etc.

Amenities were disgraceful they were signed off for being cleaned but they were not cleaned, toilet paper always on the floor across the whole long weekend and they never looked or smelt clean.

It was lucky we were amongst a group hence why we stayed but unfortunately won’t be returning due to staff on the front desk.
Good park, clean amenities and great location.

Really recommend this place. I’m an ambulatory wheelchair user so my friends and I booked the accessible cabin option. It was really great, the place was clean, tidy and comfortable; there was plenty of space to navigate inside and extra room to store the wheelchair without feeling cramped. Made it feel welcoming and not just like they were ticking the boxes to be able to list the cabin as accessible :)

The location was wonderful, right near the lake and just a short walk/wheel over to the beach, and there’s cafes nearby. There were some bumps along the paving but in general it was easy to get around the site using my wheelchair.
Great place, had a nice lake front caravan spot
Bunk beds are noisy

I'm really disappointed about my stay at this villa. The water supply has been disrupted for hours which is a huge inconvenience.And to top it off the receptionist was super rude when we talked to her about it ,no apology or anything. Seriously bad customer service. Wouldn't recommend this place to anyone.

What an stay felt safer in the NT
Gangs roaming the park belting on vans  . Security not able to cope goes on all the time is totally out of hand. Not a place to stay  with out some protection Lucky no childrem with us.
Park management do not seen to be able to control need to fence them out.
Spent 16 nights here in a caravan. The only negative is that the showers tend to be cold by mid afternoon. Great location though on the lakes and with a few minutes easy walk to the rockpool.

Oh how I wish the weather had been better for us. Torrential rain the whole three days we were here and I am so glad I booked the ensuite site! It was so nice so have a bathroom right next to the campervan. Although being winter and raining for 72 hours none of the towels dried inbetween using them. The closest campsite we could find to Sydney. 1.5 hours in on public transport either into Manly and then the ferry or the B1 bus. Very easy to get around rather than driving. We visited in August so the camp was rather empty. The reason you can’t bring dogs I’m guessing is because there are so many bunnies! So cute! Two were living under our ensuite block. Could see them hopping all over the sites at night.
Always enjoy a weekend away at Narrabeen. Highly recommended.
No dogs allowed. We have our own caravan.
Nice place to stay, hot showers and kind people.

This is a beautiful holiday park in Narrabeen NSW. We stayed here for two nights during our road trip stopover from QLD to Melbourne.

The spacious, self contained cabin was clean tidy and comfortable, with full kitchen, good hot water and room heating etc.

This location is Ideal if you prefer peaceful and tranquil surroundings away from the city atmosphere.

From this holiday park, Sydney CBD, the Opera house, Sydney botanic Gardens etc are about 37 km and approximately 60 - 90 minutes drive,  depending on the time of the day & traffic congestion etc.

As such, whilst living in a peaceful quiet atmosphere here, you are not really too far from all the attractions and activities in Sydney either.

The staff here are good too.

Negatives: This beautiful cabin had strong mouldy smell, which perhaps could be attributed to the recent heavy rain & floodings etc. We used a can of Glen 20 which helped somewhat. Otherwise, everything was hunky-dory.

Campsite was okay - the south amenities could do with some TLC but otherwise good location. Unfortunately we had some stuff stolen from our site which tainted the rest of our stay. Just a warning to be careful!
Been holidaying here for many years great place amazing views

We just stayed for two nights and I did ask to update our cabin as we were just staying in studio cabin. I said i was happy to pay more she said she wasn't sure if she could upgrade as we have checked in but who wants to hear doors shutting or people being loud. But otherwise it was good stay

Amazing caravan park with big spacious sites. We have stayed for 2 night’s, excellent location with lakeside view and for kids there is a water park and playgrounds. Our 8 year old had a wonderful time. It was an amazing experience for us.

The price seems to vary from week to week.
As with all caravan parks, there is theft.
Big clear, clean,  caravan sites. Love this spot.
Great camping and cabin camping site with good shower and toilet facilities, bbq area and activities for the whole family. Lication is also great, just a stroll to Narabeen beach
Great place to take caravan, lovely setting and a short walk to bus service that will take you into the city
Absolutely great

Myself and family needed somewhere to park our caravan last minute, we rang these guys and they were fantastic.
Absolutely amazing customer service, such a beautiful Park. All the facilities are so clean.
Will definitely be back, cannot rate them enough.
Great location , friendly staff and Wan in housekeeping was wonderful very helpful
Really nice place to stay
Great place to stay. Location is wonderful and the outlook from lakeside cabin is great.
I stayed in the caravan park for 5 nights.  Good facilities
Cabin was clean, nice and roomy, but wifi didn't work and bathroom door jammed and wouldn't shut probably, minor stuff I suppose, but standards matter.
Overall good. Janitor didn’t do a good job with the toilets though. Hosed them from a distance.

Great park quiet except on weekends. amenities could do with door maintenance due doors not being able to shut, out of alignment which would be an easy fix Warning water pressure is very strong as it blew our plumbing on our caravan to bits
Poorly organised.  People on top of each other when there are loads of free sites.  Amenities code didn't work at night. Staff had no idea.

Quaint and Clean. Loved the vibes overall.

I will be back and would recommend it to family and friends - Rolly

Fantastic location- walkable location to multiple lookouts. Great cafe in the vacinity of the park.
Very clean bathrooms and amenitites.
Lots of wildlife in the park- ducks, rabbits, bush turkeys, and native birds.

Staff were very helpful when we required assistance with our caravan.
Great camping spot with access to the north ocean pool, with a coffee shop on the way, would recommend a site with an en-suite to make camping that little bit more fun 🤩

Je ne recommande pas du tous. |J’ai du annulé mon séjour juste après l’avoir réserver. |Le personnel a répondu à mes autres mails mais pas ceux où je demande le remboursement. Ce sont des ESCROC. |C’est la honte pour un camping comme celui ci de ne pas répondre au mail de 1 et de 2 de ne pas rembourser alors que j’étais complètement dans les délais pour demander à recevoir mon argent.

Clean and efficiently run. We stayed in a bungalow it was clean but the accomodation can seem a little dated. The housekeeping staff work very hard to clean accomodation ready for new arrivals. Clean linen was provided as we stayed 8 nights. It was very quiet at night. The children loved the water park. Shame there is no shop on site it would bring in a huge income with all those guests. We left something important behind ad it was handed in for us to collect. Thank you.

Enjoyed our 3 nights here - the toilets and showers were relatively clean and modern. Really big site with lots of camp kitchens, bbqs etc. loved the location too as we went into manly and Sydney by bus / ferry easily.

The only slight let down was they’d clean the toilets at slightly inconvenient times (10am or 7pm) and you also had to use a code to enter them each time

Stayed during a long weekend and school holidays so it was originally busy. However the bins were constantly overflowing and the amenities blocks were always dirty no matter the time of day or night. Again it is busy but they charge a premium amount so this should still amount to the park being serviced when needed. Location and the area is great so somewhat of a highlight on our stay.

Best located caravan park in Sydney. A few 100 meters to the beach, ocean pool, local cafes , bus top with express bus to the city. Or bus to manly. Short walk to the shopping centre with 3 supermarkets, Kmart, JBhifi and anything else you need.
Caravan Park has many great facilities for kids and a camp kitchen with electric BBQ.
Pet owners families excluded ! Try elsewhere

First time here. Staff is so helpful that there is nothing an issue. Close to the lagoon very pretty. Great sites to see. Just a beautiful area.
Noting there are no trains here but great bus services if you want then.

very good for relaxation
, quiet area

Customer service poor and not allowed to check out late on a Sunday.
I was told the carpark inside was for long term guests only and that I couldn’t pack up and park there.  It was a Sunday and the campground seemed very quiet with few new arrivals.
Great location. Great water park for kids to play in. Rooms clean and tidy.
Great
Needs a pool!!
Very nice place
Coffee shops at front back and in park where could be better in Sydney

We stayed here in the caravan on the weekend. Booked a site months ago in advance, specifying the caravan dimensions. When we arrived the park had given us a site with a giant Norfolk Pine smack bang in the middle and the street was so narrow I couldn't reverse the caravan into the tiny spot.

I complained to reception, who told me it was the only site available, interestingly the site next to us was vacant for our entire stay, when I walked out of reception I turned around to find one of the staff laughing behind me. They obviously found my frustration quite funny.

It's not a bad park, close to the lagoon, but I was left with a bad experience due to being allocated this terrible site. Seriously the site I think it was D03 is a nightmare and isn't suitable for even a small caravan, unless you can unhitch it to maneuver onto the site.

Be warned, check Google earth beforehand to ensure the site is suitable, because the park staff obviously don't give a toss.
Lovely location.  Our rooms were very clean and a good size.  Loved their water playground for the children.
Absolutely  brilliant place for kids, especially in summer.

We stayed Lakeside and found the accomodation bellow average for the cost per night. |You only get the bare minimum provided and the cleanliness could be improved. |Maintenance should be a priority as the TV didnt work ,the furniture was in poor condition and the unit in general was not looked after.|Unfortunately i feel we could have got more for our money somewhere else|That being said the location is great, especially for families meeting here but all wanting their own space.|I think the camping option is good and reasonably priced but the experience left us disappointed

A great park in a great location!

As it is an old park the cabins are dated and a little unkempt.

It did serve the purpose and we will definitely be back

We were so excited to come back to the park because we had such a great experience the first time. But unfortunately our experience this time was horrible. We had to change our original 2 night booking to another weekend. They where accomodating and great regarding this but upon receiving an email the day of our booking they only booked us in one night not 2 we where driving 5 hours to get here. When I called they said it was my fault and that they were now booked out the second night. I accepted this and said that fine but I need a refund of our money for the second night. I then told her we would need late check in. She said she would text me the details for late check in and that there would be an envelope with my name on it in the safe. When got there and still had not received any late check in details. I called the after hours number and got the combination for the safe and when I opened it they didn’t have an envelope with my name on it! I had to open someone else’s to get the code for the bathrooms so we could at least use the toilet. Thankfully I remembered the number of the site she told me.

Small things like this can really ruin a family’s small holiday and it definitely impacted us. Our son was devastated, we had to go home early. We paid additional kennel fees for our dogs which we can get refunded. Such bad service, I honestly couldn’t believe it.

A good park in a nice location in Narrabeen. Facilities in the park and the local amenities were all good. Staff were very helpful with maps and suggestions in the local area plus suggestions on how to get to Sydney.

We had a family holiday here in Feb '25. Overall, we loved it. The lagoon is adjacent to the beach, meaning the little ones could have a great day as well as the adults who wanted to swim in the surf.  The facilities are great within the park. There is a lovely water park for the kids, as well as a great shared BBQ & tables area which is under cover.  The shared bathroom block is kept clean and there is even a charming lady running a great coffee cart on some of the days of the week.

The staff in reception were super helpful & cheery. The pity was that due to some unforeseen cleaning staff no-shows we could not even enter the park for an hour and a half on checkin day - you need an allocated space before they will give you a code, so nope, we waited in the hot sun outside while they cleaned our waiting cabin.

Next issue was that we had some not-working appliances in one of our two cabins (the AC & the fridge) which meant that it became stifling & unmanageable due to the glorious hot weather. Back to the trusty reception staff, who quickly moved half of our number to another cabin. Issues solved!

That night we accidentally locked our one and only key inside. Within 3 minutes the friendly security staff had dropped over & unlocked our door for us, as we stood by, sheep-faced & grateful.

I highly recommend this park as a gorgeous spot to unwind & relax. It is not a 5-star resort, but you don't pay resort prices either. It is simple, functional and so relaxed. The Convection Microwave was amazing!
Great place to stay in your van very clean amenities and staff
I absolutely love narrabeen!! An bridge a bit easier an accessible I guess?
Very close to the lagoon and the ocean, which is enjoyable. But no shadow at all on the campsites and the bathrooms codes were not working well.
Very nice camping with clean and new amenities. But not too many trees and no shadows
We decided to camp in our tent & had a fabulous time. The park is family friendly, great location and value for money. We will definitely return.

Der Campingplatz ist sehr gross. Die WCs und Duschen sind sauber, auf den Parzellen liegt teilweise etwas Müll was schade aber wohl kaum zu vermeiden ist. ||Der Platz ist mit den öV ca. eine Stunde von Sydney entfernt aber sehr gut zu erreichen. Die Busstation ist ca. 5 Minuten vom Campingplatz entfernt.
Great location, lots to do for families onsite and in the immediate surroundings. Being a family park it can be quite noisy but that's part of the charm

Worst holiday park we've stayed.|I don't normally leave reviews but I feel this is the one I must share.|The room was not ready by 2. Why not arranging more staffs if it gets busy rather than advertising 2pm guarantee check in? We were only able to get in after 3pm. |When we checked in, the room we originally booked had a very strong urine smell in one of the rooms and there was no way we could put our kids to sleep in that room. When I told the receptionist, instead of being apologetic she was rude. No one wants to unpack, pack and unpack while managing 4 kids. They eventually downgraded us to a room with cheaper rates. |Pans pots plates are all disgusting. Bunk bed is so old it needs to be disposed right away. Because of the noise it makes our kids cannot sleep in as smallest move they make the whole place can hear it. It doesn't look steady too. They are sleeping on the sofa tonight. |Stay at Umina Ocean Beach NRMA or Big4 and stay away from this place. Zero maintenance and I wouldn't even give 1 star.

On January 31st, around 11:30 PM, a group of kids, about 12 years old, caused significant damage in the men’s bathroom. They tore all the soap dispensers off the walls, dumped soap all over the floor, and filled one of the empty bottles with water. Then, they threw the bottle over the top of the shower stall I was in, and it nearly hit my head as it fell to the floor. Frankly, I felt pretty shaken by the behavior of these unruly kids. Earlier, around 11 PM, I also heard loud screaming from some young people, and with everything that happened in the bathroom, I couldn’t sleep the whole night. I strongly suggest improving security, especially during peak hours for the restrooms and showers. It would also be helpful to install password locks on the restrooms and other park facilities, build fences, have night patrols, and take additional steps to enhance safety.

Sadly this place is run down the cabins are looking very tired the grounds look like a gardener attents the the bare basics it looks very messy and the amenities aren't that great compared to most other caravan parks they rely to much on the natural beauty of narrabeen to see them thru the massive price hikes they continually impose and they give very little back for those increases they haven't improved the waterpark since inception there is mo pool because the know people will go to the beach or the rock pool (which becomes disgustingly dirty and should be cleaned more than once a week especially thru summer .

Over the weekend we tried camping for the first time. Staying at the NRMA Lakeside @nrmaparksandresorts at Narrabeen was amazing - you can camp, stay in cabins and use all the facilities like a park, water splash, on site coffee, lakeside and local beach as well as plenty of local restaurants and cafe. We couldn’t be more impressed with the level of service, communication and cleanliness. Honestly whenever I went to the bathroom there was a cleaner!

Our favourite place to stay in NSW! Beautiful Narrabeen Lake, wonderful kids activities every day leading up to Christmas and throughout January. It is easy to get to travel to Sydney and other coasts from the caravan park and supermarkets are also local. Friendly staff, clean park and nice facilities. Just missing a pool and/or updated play equipment.
We stay here every year in our caravan. The facilities are great and the kids love the go karts and the water play. Definitely a great family place to stay and a nice walk to the beach.

I have been coming to this place for more than 20 years its a family tradition my family has been coming here for over 45 years  even before when they didn't have cabins the cabins are old and dirty they need a revamp bad you would think they would have done something when they were closed for four months but year after year its getting worse and worse I come here now just to keep my family tradition alive but every year its getting harder and harder and more expansive the money you spend for 10 days here you could by a return flight to Europe the complaints fall on deaf ears and being a Gold member with nrma for over 50 years you think they would do something about but they never do so this review is aimed at nrma get your act together with this caravan park update it there are so many other big 4 caravan parks that are so much better and cheaper and its like the narrabeen park has been left behind and its sad cause I want to keep my family tradition please do something nrma

Just want to start off with how rude the staff were. They just cram everyone into the on site spots. Cars parked everywhere and they don't even care. Makes it hard for people to put in there caravans. Such a narrow street as it is.

Veel te dure camping

Geen zwembad
Geen winkel
Geen restaurant

Kale camping
Slecht beveiligd je kan zo vanaf de lagoon de camping op lopen

120 dollar voor 1 persoon camper 5 meter
1 nacht

Do not go went during a week that it was meant to rain a sure enough it did rain but our equipment was good for heavy rain so when it rained the next day we thought we would be ok but at 2am in the morning we got woken up to our tent flooded because our plot of land we were given got atleast 30mm of rain causing over $1000 worth of damage and when I called to ask for a refund I was told it was my fault because our equipment wasn't set up properly and wasn't designed for rain ignoring the hole they put us in with no warning as we were told by other campers it had happend the week prior and then we called again a second time and the manager Emily hung up and continued to interrupt me while talking. update they gave me a $255 refund which was no where near the almost $700 I paid for 5 days and the manager Emily has banned me from the park

Great park and place.
Disappointed about not allowing a floor in your annex. Due to rain  water pooled in annexe and flooded all out gear.
Camp kitchens excellent. Toilets cleaned twice a day.

Lakeside Villa - none of the internal door have locks including bedrooms, shower and toilet. Renaissance, Baroque, Byzantine ... there have been many great architectural movements ... now we have the "Semi-public display of bowel movement" movement.
If an adult wants to scratch their head standing in the bedroom they are advised to have ceiling fan on 1 or less ... that way you'll only need a band-aid not surgery ... the ceiling fans are quite low.
The Villas only have 1 bin ... no recycle bin which is disappointing.
They take liberties with my mobile number by sending it nagging emails about going 8Km/h etc. Pi55 off NRMA ... that's not why I gave you my mobile number.

Awesome location, an amazing region. Very large park, great sized sites. Run by Northern Beaches Shire Council, under the NRMA Parks banner. it is a very busy park, which would be generating a handsome income for Council, but they clearly need to invest more of that, back into the infrastructure. Repairs & maintenance appear to be a low priority. Amenities are very dated, and dirty. There needs to be more paths, concrete areas, outside showers etc. for people, so they are not dragging dirt into the amenities. A whole new amenities Reno. required. Sites are well mowed, however, need a makeover. Compacted ground needs to be regrassed, with large dirt areas repatriated. Zero slab sites available, which need to be added over time. I trust that this is taken as positive feedback 🙏

Lovely spot to take the caravan close to beach & Lake.
Was disappointed in the condition of the park. Never saw one maintenance or cleaning staff over the 3 days we were there. Wood around outside of amenities block in poor condition. Place needs work.
Great caravan park, close to beach and lake. Unfortunately the unpowered spots were only for tents, but instead our spot was right next to the amenities. Everything was clean and neat.

This is a top park. Very family friendly with plenty of family activities available. Safe lake for swimming and canoeing, close to beach and shops, bike hire and so much more. Great place for the kids, from toddler to teenagers. Was very busy when we were there and the amenities got a bit untidy, but if you consider the huge number of people using them, this is understandable. They were cleaned and tidy when we returned later in the day. Would recommend if your looking for a top spot close to Sydney that has everything you could expect.
We will return.
Great spot to stop on the way down the east coast, before tackling the Sydney traffic. Beautiful beach cross road and safe swimming in the inlet.

Rooms were pricey considering the rooms were pretty old and dirty, we checked out 3 hours after checking in and drove straight home, when we went to go complain the lady at the desk was on the phone whilst my husband and other customers were waiting a while that we just walked out. Was pretty disappointed that there was no swimming pool, all holiday parks we stayed at all have pool, this one just had a cheap little splash park where you have to continuously press a button to turn on after a few minutes. Pretty disappointed at this one. We usually love all the holiday parks.
Nice park ,with the lake and the beach makes it perfect for all young and old., must admit the rats 🐀 in the evening have a party looking for a feed........not very pleasant for some 🤔😅
Loved it, this is one of many visits, we will definitely be back again!
Beautiful location..... rude staff.... most NRMA's are pet friendly... but not this one. Also no cancelations or changes to bookings for no reason whatsoever.
Friendly staff, great location right on the water and near other places/public transport and affordable with other activities available on the park grounds! What more could you want?

Great location to base yourself to get into Sydney in a camper as there aren’t many campsites too close to the city. We liked the facilities here and found the staff to be helpful when we decided to stay and extra night. Can get quite busy but the toilets/shower blocks remained clean.

Too many smokers.  Should have smoking areas so the rest of us don't suffer. Butts everywhere.
A lot of riff raff who do not follow caravan park etiquette.  Maybe a handout should be given to each vehicle on entry.
Will not stay here again if things don't improve.
No response to the issue of the butts at A16 which I reported.
It seems Management only respond to positive views
bbq grills, microwave and stove in common area. really friendly hosts. full of campers. on site coffee truck fri-sun. 5 min walk to rock pool, the best we have experienced in nsw.

👎Couldn't be more disappointed  👇

#1  We paid more to have water views but the big glass doors were FILTHY and couldn't get a nice view at all.

#2   Then water kept turning off and twice was left in the shower waiting for the water to come back on.

#3   Couldn't wash up cause the water was off and also didn't have a plug for the sink.

#4   The toilet paper was like it was from a cheap country council public toilets 🚻

#5   The outside lights that stay on shine right into the bedroom window so our room wasn't dark and made it hard to sleep.

#6  Had to move the bed as our bubs dummy went under it, and it was in need of a decent clean and vacuum, lollie and rubbish underneath

BUT the beds were comfortable, shower did have good pressure and the unit was set up well.

And bloody possums on the roof😡🤬
Great location, rooms and facilities looking a little tired. Poorly managed bookings process however, once checked in we had a great time at a wonderful location.
Best damn big 4 anywhere.... recommendations for the camping and all dorms.

It’s our fourth time to booked here for powered site. We loved this place every time we have holiday since we came from Brisbane. Walking distance to catch bus stop going to Manly to catch ferry to the city and vice versa. I highly recommend this place especially you came from interstate.

Great caravan park,  stayed twice in two weeks.
The coffee from the store was excellent 😀

We checked in today at the lakeside. Not the first time and  this is probably one of our favorites holiday place, only 1hr drive from home. Perfect place to enjoy the lake and swimming at the rockpool

I was very disappointed with the receptionist that served me. She looked Indian(not to be racist, I worked with 95%of them at work), didn't greet me and her face just so sour. Very rude and she gave me the check in paper and didn't even show me where to go. I have to turn back and ask. When I asked about the wifi passport, she just turned the paper and told me all the information is there.

I will never want to be served by her again, better wait for friendly person. If they can't have good manners, they shouldn't be at the front office.
Very noisy at night, can’t sleep at all. People drinking and playing music very loudly outdoor until 3am.

Love the clean spacious villa with one ensuite and a spare room with bunk beds and single bed.  Loved the fact that it also had a spare toilet.  Living area and kitchen is spacious.  Kitchen is nicely equipped with essential utensils.  What would be great to include are:  a floor mat at the entrance to dust off shoes after our return from the beach and a roll of kitchen paper towel in the kitchen.  The rooms are not serviced if one stays there for less than 7 days.  So having a pack of floor wipes in the kitchen and a bottle of harpic in the toilet will be very useful to help us maintain cleanliness and keeping things tidy.  The Reception signage on entry is not very clearly visible because it's almost obliterated by plants. A clear and large signboard at the top would be useful.  The staff at reception are a bit robotic and I feel they just do the bare minimum, "tick the box" kind of exercise.  They don't offer any additional tips. For example, they presume everyone knows the way to the beach or to the closest supermarket, chemist etc.  I found these out quickly by myself but offering these tips to guests would help staff go that extra mile and reflect a genuine interest in their customers.  A regular shuttle service just to the beach and Woolworths would also be incredibly valuable.  Love this place and it's great value for money.

Incredibly impressed with my entire stay.
From the moment I booked to the moment I left.
The location was fantastic, but even more so the staff were amazing. I had to move sites at one stage and the staff were so helpful in accommodating me, even upgraded me for the rest of my stay.
I can’t think everyone enough for such a great experience. Highly recommend.

I had a very disappointing experience here. The staff was unfriendly and unwelcoming from the start. Despite being advertised as family-friendly, it’s anything but! Children are not allowed to play independently, which really defeats the purpose of a family outing. To make matters worse, we were constantly reminded about the rules, making the whole visit feel restrictive and uncomfortable. Definitely not a place I’d recommend for families.

We always thoroughly enjoy our stay at Lakeside! It's a short 35 minutes drive for us for a night away from the City! It can get pretty noisy with kids running around and on bikes etc but you kind of expect it in a campsite like this. Facilities are always clean and available.
Had a lakeside spot for my caravan, awesome to say the least

Receptionist not friendly hardly spoke not welcoming, cabin dated needs update. Exhaust fan Beth room noisy needs replacement. Parking near cabin difficult after traversing a narrow pedestrian path with many cars parked in narrow spaces.
We had workers gurnying next door for a whole morning.  Way too loud we had to get out.

Such a beautiful place to stay.
The beach and the lake are so nice to be close to and a lovely cafe at 4knots.
Would be a great idea to have a courtesy bus to the bus stops for those not able to walk distances and a change machine for the washing machine and dryer.
The whole place is run down, it looks like the handyman is on a holiday, not there though.
Great trip!

Logged onto pet friendly caravan parks Sydney. North Narambeen came up. On Wednesday 28th August sent in an E-mail to confirm as have a small dog. No reply, so drove the 380Kms down to Sydney Lake Side Holiday Park the following day. On arrival was greeted by a rather large sign stating, Dogs Prohibited.
You may need to update your website to keep the travelling public informed of your policy.

Very old cabins. They need to refurbish not cheap. Camp|Kitchen ordinary no tools supplied. Did the job. We washed up in the bathroom. We were surprised at a premium location how ordinary things were. Did the job for two nights. Maybe they they need to update the camp kitchens

Loved our stay here..one of my favourite spots on our big lap of Aus. Would love to come back in the summer. Love the family bathroom,allowing me to bath bubs easier. The view of the lake and lovely walks nearby are just gorgeous.
Great location great customer service

DOG NOT ALLOWED !!!!
Although it asks online how many pets you have when booking and will take your money when stating you have a pet, they are not allowed. It is also the first caravan park that comes up when you search ‘dog friendly caravan park Sydney’
We booked months in advance while going on a trip around Australia and were very excited about this place. When I rung to confirm we were on our way I was informed dogs are not allowed and then was told I’d need to pay a $30 admission free to cancel!! This is outrageous to me considering we booked online and then had a 3 minute conversation to cancel and it cost $30???
Very very disappointing, would not recommend this place!
Camping ground was in excellant condition and very good service.
一切都很滿意
Beautiful clean park on the Narabeen Lagoon and a short stroll to the ocean.  Great facilities and very helpful and friendly staff!
I went to visit a friend who had his caravan there for a few days. my first time. i was so impressed with all the facilities and cleanliness and top lakeside location.

We decided to stay here as we had to drop one of our vehicles for a service nearby and we live 96km away, the opposite side of Sydney, which would have involved being stuck in peak hour traffic for a lot of the trip, so instead of it being approx 1hr 45 minutes, it would likely have been about 3 hours!  Having decided to travel up and stay nearby it seemed sensible to take a second vehicle so that we had some way of getting around while the other was in being serviced (and we had no idea how long that might take!)  On arrival, I asked about parking onsite for the second vehicle and was told in a not particularly pleasant manner that only one vehicle was allowed onsite and we would have to park the other in a street outside the park!  Once we were onsite, we found that there was a large carpark just inside the park which was only half full overnight and many vacant tent and caravan spots, as well as the parking outside the studio we had had NO other cars beside ours!  There would have been absolutely no reason not to allow us to have our second vehicle within the holiday park, other than blind insistence on following a rule.  I can understand that over holiday periods and summer, there would be no room for additional vehicles, however, on a Monday night in winter, this should not have been an issue.  If you are travelling with more that one vehicle, I would suggest looking for alternative accommodation, although there does not appear to be much in the way of hotels/motels/caravan parks, etc in this locality.
Great place to stay close to Sydney
A great place to stay everything is easy walk including lake, ocean, restaurants and shopping centre

Due to circumstance I recently had to stay here for a week.

Over $70 a night for a grass site, no slab, and slopping about in the sandy mud.

Toilets and showers were only ever vaguely cleaned.

Showers cold every time in the ladies.

I'm travelling Australia, and from real experience this is a $35 a night park,  stretching $40 a night. But those parks had a slab at least.

This place is a prime example of corporate greed.

The NRMA are meant to be about its members, and the community according to their corporate About Us page.

However,  actions speak louder than words. With people financially stressed, the NRMA is now at 70% of a whopping $100 a night for a dirt and grass site.  For ONE night.

For the price of a half a week at these rates, I could fly to a south east Asian holiday.

We can see why Australian's choose to spend their hard saved money overseas, when their local "mutual company" is screwing the community and memebers at these insane rates.

Shame on the NRMA. Fly to Asia instead. It's cheaper than paying this corporate behemoth who clearly care nothing,  with the prices they are charging.

I visited NRMA Sydney Lakeside Holiday Park with friends and family for a camping trip, and it was a fantastic experience. The park is a great place for kids, offering many activities including a fun water play area. With both a lake and beach nearby, there's no shortage of outdoor adventures. The facilities are well-maintained.  The friendly atmosphere and variety of amenities made our stay enjoyable and memorable. Highly recommended for a fun family getaway!
Love these holiday parks operated by a mutual. That means 'for the benefit of the members', not for private profiteers.
Bad news. Expect good computer knowledge to fill in poor app. Most power sockets burnt due to use of 10 amp plugs instead of 15 amp. Very impersonal.

Great spot, lake on one side and surf beach on the other, the Rock poll is also there plus shower facilities. Caravan ensuite with concrete slab best results for level and keep the area clean from dirt. Only disappointment is the area space is very small, compared to other parks...
Easy to bus, not far from shops and beach pool
Don't stay here if you are afraid of rats because they are everywhere at night time.
Fabulous location but nowhere near as clean as I would like it to be.  Policy  for refunds is very inflexible....even with notice. Would only book daily.
Favourite place to stay, amazing view, friendly staff, great location

Loved our stay here. Great location and fantastic park, I would have given it 5 stars but I juts can’t based on the bedding we had in our villa. We stayed in a lovely waterfront villa, and I would start again however, the bed and the pillows are horrific. Soo uncomfortable, for the price there really should be a more comfortable bed and better quality pillows. Bed is hard as a rock and pillows flat as flat can be. Only thing that let it down. We had to go to Kmart and buy pillows it was that bad! Woke up with sore necks! Hopefully management can take that on board. Other than that perfect stay!
Great location & choice of accommodation

Wir haben auf einem der Zeltplätze unser Nachtlager aufgeschlagen. Die Plätze waren grosszügig, das Personal freundlich, die Toiletten und Duschen sauber und ordentlich.
Wir waren im Herbst vor Ort und somit nicht in der Hochsaison.

A very relaxing and enjoyable stay. My studio cabin was very spacious and clean. Staff were exceptional in their friendliness and professionalism. Great location close to the beach and lagoon with an array of cafes nearby. Highly recommend.

We stayed for a couple of nights in the caravan park. The amenities were very good, there was lots of space and the staff were helpful and friendly. The best thing about the park is the location, right on beach. It was glorious and worth staying for alone.

The camp has a great location. The beach is just across the road. The camp has a indoor and outdoor kitchen, laundry, playground for the kids, you can rent bikes etc. Toilets are clean. Only the weekends get busy and bit noisy.
Great except for the booking form that will only accept an Australian phone number and an Australian home address - not good for non Australians
A fabulous place to spend a few days in our van mid week.

30 minutes ago I found myself accidentally driving into the park due to the barrier being open. Upon realizing my mistake, I immediately sought assistance to exit the park. Unfortunately, my interaction with the reception staff during this situation was far from pleasant. The young girl I dealt with came across as unfriendly and less than helpful, which added to the stress of the situation.
While I understand that everyone can have an off day, I hope this feedback serves as a constructive reminder of the importance of kindness and professionalism in customer service. A more understanding and helpful approach would have significantly improved the experience
I don’t give five stars out much, but this place was really nice. I’d have to recommend it and we’ll definitely stay here again.

评分下来不是没有理由的，设施很老，儿童娱乐区也很小。公共厕所和浴室有点小，公共洗衣机很老了，洗完孩子的衣服都长疹子。园区围栏形同虚设，有密码的门不远处就没有围栏什么人都可以进园区，搞不明白建设费花到哪里去了。

第一次来的时候混订的ENSUITE和POWERSITE，员工很耐心地帮我们调整好到最后不用挪位置定在一个ENSUITE。

好在有个小的WATERLAND可以让孩子们玩一下，撑一撑台面。优点是离海很近，不远处有个半自然泳池，不过不适合小孩子。
The location is amazing, 5 minutes walk to the beach, the lake is just a few steps away. The staff is friendly and helpful as well. Can’t wait to go back.

Atrocious!!!! Bungalow cabin dirty! Key not ready until well after 2pm and beds x3 in second bedroom not made and paid premium prices. I have stayed in some questionable places but I have always had a bed made. I wouldn’t expect you to stay at my accomodation and make your own bed.
Great holiday place to bring the kids.
Recommend to all
fantastic
Rozbitá pračka,nefunkční sušička,nepříliš čisté sprchy
Still a great holiday park, in a wonderful location.
Really great location, friendly staff and nice clean room. The price could be a bit more reasonable but it could be that it's more expensive during summer.

Been here a few times, definitely a nice spot to stay. Plenty for the kids to do and so close to everyone you need.
All facilities you need are on site and clean.

We came just after Dec School holidays, the camp was still full. The service at reception impeccable and highly efficient. Communication from the team in the lead up to my arrival is next level. Love it ...access to other amenities close by is great ....swim in the lake 10 meters away, walk to the beach, 200 meters....shopping centres a 5 min drive. Love it will be back. Not to forget, the campsites are big, cater for my 8.5 m caravan with ease

The Bungalows are very good and comfortable, minutes walk to the beach, we live the facilities in site, specially the ice cream vending machine and the other amenities.
Highly recommended for all kind of families

Firstly the best caravan park ,and a bonus to it is the friendly  and experience staff , I know it's a difficult  job dealing with holidays makers, but staff never disappoint , we tell everyone  that we meet about Lakeside .
Thank you.

We've just spent 5 days here and loved it.  The location is mickey mouse with the beach and rock pool 400mtrs away.  Amenities are lovely and there's plenty for little ones if you have them.  Home now but wishing we were still there.
Great camping ground

A fantastic place to stay without going too far from home for Sydney locals. Right on the lake, which is amazing, with cafes and grocery shopping within walking distance. The beach is right there, but when the beach weather isn't great, the lake is such a handy, family-friendly option for paddle boarding, fishing or just floating around. We were camping and the campsites are such a generous size, we had no problems fitting in a full hybrid camper, 2 swags, 2 shelters and a 4WD. We had ridiculous rain one night (80mm!!) and no issues with flooding or drainage, so happy with the grassy sites. Just be mindful it is a camp ground that is very accessible to the public, so keep things locked up. There was a security presence at night which was good, but be aware people can wander in and out quite easily.

The only suggestion I have is that it'd be great to have a card operated laundry, rather than having to have cash and go to Reception to get it changed to $1 coins. This was quite frustrating when you're camping for a week with a family, so obviously need to wash. The facilities were ample and I had no trouble getting access to a washer or drier, it would just be easier to be able to tap a card as needed. Otherwise, you have to wait for Reception to be open and have cash with you, which is getting rarer and rarer these days!

Overall, a fabulous place to stay for a family camping getaway. Allowing dogs would make it perfect! 🐶😉
Stay here a lot and never disappointed. Such a great place

Love camping at narrabeen! A family tradition for generations in my extended family going back 100 years and started by my 2nd great grandmother. It’s a great location, close to all the beaches and shops, bus stop nearby, everyone is friendly and easy access to nearby cafes. I enjoy returning each year to see my family which is a blessing. The caravan park would be even better with better access to disabled facilities, better lighting at nighttime especially throughout the caravan park, a pool for guests and more family activities throughout the day and evening.

Love this place. We stayed in cabin. And its like a home away from home with access to cafes and restaurants close by. Beach, lake, water play area and kids zone, daily activities kept kids busy.
Access to beach and lake  (for young kids)
Kids loved it.
Clean rooms beautiful spot
Great feel and friendly atmosphere.

Great location. Close to North Narrabeen rockpool, beach and lagoon.

Also close to nearby shops and public transport which is great.

Some staff are very friendly whilst others need to be taught some customer service skills such as the older grounds keeper who did not speak friendly when asking questions and just ran around in his buggy.mist if the day watching people with glaring eyes.

Issues are as follows:
> Rat infestation is really bad
> Collection of garbage during Christmas was woeful as many bins filling up for about 3 days before being collected
> Toilet facilities mainly the dedicated and seperate disabled toilet on the south side was out of order without explanation
> Toilets continually leaking after flushing and maintenance staff not willing to repair
> Wi-Fi is non existent half the time and considering father in-law isa on powered caravan site paying $3k for a month there would be service but nope
> Some sites like on D, E and F rows do flood when there is a lot of rain. Have mentioned this many times to management both current and previous but nothing get sorted to fix it
> Whilst there is a small water park for kids, a swimming pool for those who may struggle to get into the lagoon or the rockpool for people with disabilities would be very much appreciated
> Accessibility is also an issue as whilst there is disabled changing facilities, the gutters getting to paths is quite steep and need to be modified to meet DDA compliance
> Better lighting would also need to be placed around as some parts are really dark walking through
> This used to be a great place to stay but over the last 12-24 months standards have very much slipped
Awesome place..
Very expensive

Great place but has a very bad vermin and pest problem.

We had lots of rats scurrying around our feet, the campervan and literally into other peoples tents at dusk and during the night. They also have rabbits and a large hornets nest right by the access to the central facilities. Its only time until a kid gets badly stung. Great location but the rats are a big problem.

Staff, service and location is amazing. Right on the lake, lagoon and 2 mins walk to the beach and natural pools. The spot is perfect, just wished it had a pool onsite for the little kids to enjoy.
The cabin we stayed in was very outdated and could really use a make-over. The vanity in the bathroom leaked when on and the drawers are tight and could barely open, therefore couldnt use. The carpets must be original and look 100 years old.
The amenities throughout the park were great, clean and very accessible. Cleaning staff were also very friendly always greeting us whenever we crossed paths.
No internet service and the wifi did not reach out cabin, which was a shame.
But overall the location of the park outweighed it all and kept us very busy throughout the days.
We just stayed one night in a cabin and it was lovely to stay there. Cabin was clean. Nice walk from the park to beach, just across the road.
Great caravan park,  great location,  helpful staff,  clean amenities  - recommended!

Very convenient location with coffee shop and beach across the road and supermarket 5 mins drive away.

Kids loved the play equipment, waterpark and hiring little pedal cars to drive around.

The cabins were clean and neat with everything we needed (except a vegetable peeler, plus we needed extra dishwashing liquid and kitchen towels, but the office could probably have provided all of those things)

The oval just near the caravan park is sometimes host to markets, so we unexpectedly got to go to the Christmas markets. Bonus!

Great weekend away!

Just a note: the beach at the lagoon tends to have random oyster shells and rocks mixed in with the sand. We did a lot of digging in the sand and ended up with a few cuts as we encountered oyster shells. Bring some little beach spades to save everyone's fingers (or bring band aids and disinfectant 😆).
Great caravan park for a stopover to go into city or zoo, everything was clean and kids loved the water park

Camped on a powered tent site mid November. The campsite is superbly run. Toilet and shower facilities are well maintained and kept clean. Kids splash park was a hit.

Check out is 10am by a 12pm late check out is available if needed and a half day rate of $40 if you wanna start on until 5.30pm. Just pop into the office to organise it.

The only negative of our camping trip was the location of our campsite. We were situated right at the very back of the northern camp ground. The free WiFi did not extend to and we were right beside the road so could hear all the cars drive by; well past midnight and in the early hours of the morning.

If you don't wanna cook there are two cafes at the entrances and a coffee/light snack food caravan on site. Narrabeen shops is only a 2 min drive where you'll find a supermarket, pizza, sushi, Thai and hot chicken shop.

We have already booked our next stay there.

Not the safest place. I had my phone stolen there. Went to the toilets and put it on the toilet roll holder. My fault, I left it there. Realised, when I got back to the tent that I had left it behind. Went back and it was gone. No one handed it in. The toilets were pin only access. So it was either cleaners or someone staying there. Not a nice feeling knowing someone is a tea leaf, and it is someone in the camp.

My daughter and 2 grandchildren and myself were very excited to see each other and have a mini break as we don’t see each other very much as we live along distance apart, sadly when we got to the park the cabin we had booked was the bunker one,  little did I know that is ALL IT IS! No kitchen,no chair,NO KETTLE, NO CUPS, NO MINI FRIDGE NOTHING! For the price it seriously was disgusting, I have stayed at many caravan parks and this has never happened before. Really disappointing and the toilet smelt like a truck stop. We also had a punch up fight outside our cabin at night, no security and felt unsafe. WOULD NOT RECOMMEND, VERY SLACK ACCOMMODATION!

Von dort aus mit dem Bus und der Fähre in die Stadt. Ganz einfach.
Stellplatz angenehm und ruhig.

Lovely campsite close to the ocean and a lake. Eventhough your very close to the city, its very quiet. Set up pretty big so lots of space. But that might have been the current occupancy.

Annebelle from the frontdesk was very friendly and helpful when extending our stay with 2 nights.

Would come back 😁
el lugar está muy bueno, el parque está en condiciones, pero te tienen que gustar los niños, hay muchos corriendo, en bici y jugando por ahi

A nice campground close to the lagoon. Facilities are good but not quite the same as other NRMA campgrounds.
You camp directly on the grass or soil without gravel / drainage solution but the soil is great and probably drains well. The pine trees offer lots of shade but lose sticky pine sap. Our tent was full of it and it attracts lots of ants which attracted spiders.
We booked with a group but they've put us in the opposite corner of the park for some reason which was quite disappointing. It was also pretty loud until about 10.30pm so not the best if you come with young kids. Sunday morning all but 2 toilets were blocked and beer cans everywhere.
Very spacious camping area with loads of amenities and things to enjoy your stay
Cabins need a makeover. Loose fitting, leaking shower. Location is perfect with lagoon and beach.

Caravan camping. Unfortunately the Ladies showers were cold for2 days. Sadly the shower block floors were muddy most of the time  following rainy weather. There were no facilities for mopping the floor after use.
Otherwise toilets & showers were efficient.
Very nice, clean and relaxed place to go and camping

Beautiful park, well situated, amazing staff and wifi all over the park. We are in a coaster bus in an unpowered site yet still have a little luxury of wifi. Right across from a Beautiful beach. Well worth the stay
Camp site right next to the Lake
Wonderful place for camping.. very near to beach and lakeside park keeps you cool during summer. Very well organized even for unpowered sites. Camp kitchen and bathrooms are very well maintained.
We had a great stay here. So much to do and being so close to Manly and the Ferry to the Quay we found it easy to keep busy.

Lake side park has the best view of Narrabeen, the service is very good the cabins been all refurbished and painted and the cleaner standing are better then ever , been given a new refresh for this summer , the team are very friendly and helpful, the prices are very affordable for the location , the place is perfect for kids very safe and fun . Lots of activities around , beside to the beach and the lake , bike area , walk area , wild animals , bbq areas , lots and lots of good things  to do , is 2 coffee place very close , the perfect place for families make memories , they offer wifi . I’m sure you going to have s nice and happy time there ! Best place at northern beach’s ⭐️
Dirty showers, bins overflowing, camp kitchen and fridge equally filthy. Staff friendly but we ended up using the showers at the anytime fitness.

Dissatisfied, poor customer service.

My sister stayed at a Cabin, the bathroom had mould on ceiling and on the blind. It's by far worst NRMA park we have ever stayed.

Staff we dealt with  were abrupt, total opposite of our past experience with NRMA parks.

My son's family is between houses for 2 weeks.
Woo Hoo.

Great price for the place. Very easy to get around. And right near the beach. Very kid friendly. Rooms are clean and they keep the place very clean overall. Very friendly staff. And their websites are very easy to use. Thanks

On checking in the staff member advised identification had to be provided for the reason that NRMA Sydney Lakeside Holiday Park shelters victims of domestic violence.  We have stayed here before but this is the first time the reason for identification has been mentioned.  While not ideal to broadcast this here, fellow guests equally deserve to be aware this is the case.  The owners are showing a severe lack of respect to guests by not being transparent about these circumstances.  Guests should be advised at the time of booking that this is the case, thereby allowing them to make an informed decision as to whether they are happy to assume the risk of possibly exposing themselves to potentially violent situations if interactions occur with park intruders.  Naturally, this risk could be extreme.  Having said that, it is a nonsense that identification has to be provided by guests for this reason as there is no security in place to stop intruders simply walking in from the foreshore.
Great for family vacation
Fantastic location, clean amenities and large sites! This is well run park and worth the money!
Nice and quiet. Reasonable price

Fantastic Big 4 Park. Walking distance to the Narrebeen beach, shops and lake.
Excellent facilities, well planned setting. Good lighting at night. Security codes for the amenities,  games rooms and exit from park. Good manicured areas. Friendly staff.

This is a hidden gem of a camp ground. Nearby to beautiful narrabeen  beach.
Ensuite caravan site was great for the family.
There is a great cafe nearby and also a food truck on-site with great coffee.
Clean amenities and nice staffs
Great park.  2 year old loves the water park. Needs  bouncy pillow for kids. Great location on the beach and nice cafes walking distance.
Nice and clean camping spot. Getting there is a bit like a maze through the streets. Also when calling ahead was quoted a different price than what paid. But overall nice spot
Great spot. Fantastic coffee across the road at Nourished Cafe. Loved it. Clean. Great staff
Love this place
Super friendly and accommodating staff. Great location right next to the beach and river. Cabin was clean and had everything we needed for a fun holiday by the sea.

Arrived for the first time,staff great,huge sites for vans and lots to do.
Would recommend this park to all.

Right from the outset of checking in, I had previously booked by phone which I find beneficial to get the best rate and possibly a more convenient site that suits your needs better.
I had booked a powered caravan site for this stay. Two minutes walk to the Lakes edge and eight minutes to the rock pool. The amenities are kept immaculate during my stay.

If you want to just be lazy and stroll around the local area, grab a coffee or two then this is the perfect destination, or maybe drive down to Manly Wharf which is thirty minutes drive from the NRMA Lakeside Park along Ocean Rd. Who knows you may want to catch the Sydney Harbour ferry over to Circular Quay.

Fishing, or riding your bike will keep you amused and maybe a walk along the ocean beach will give you a well-deserved appetite from a local Cafe or a BBQ dinner at your campsite.

Enjoy your holiday that's the most important thing.
Regards
good

Stayed 2 nights (Sat&Sun) on a powered site at the end of May, and while it was busier than I expected for off peak, there was plenty of std powered sites available.
Didn't use any of the playground equipment but the amenities were clean and well maintained.
My gripe is, despite there being plenty of sites available to spread people out, it seemed like the management wanted to jam people next to one another. Also despite there being plenty of spots about, people were tenting on the island between "main street",  the access in and out of the park.  Perhaps this is necessary during the peak of summer holidays when the park is bulging at the seams, but a standard weekend in May?
